entry.title: All My Life
entry.summary: Hello there. Today, I'd like to talk about love. When I…
entry.content:
Hello there.

Today, I'd like to talk about love.

When I was dying, or rather being poisoned to kill the thing trying to kill me before either killed me, I remember driving.

Up and down.

An hour each way.

Over and over.

To the poison centers that were trying to heal me.

And I remember, during one of these times, listening to a movie.

I didn't watch, just listened.

And it was about an fit Asian man.

Who loved to cook.

Who used embarrassing bravery to meet the girl of his dreams.

And say hello.

They fell in love.

And he got sick.

And, it didn't end well for him.

But she was so strong.

And I remember listening and thinking, "Is this how my story ends?"

And I remember, getting so angry.

So angry.

Fuq dat.

And it helped me. 

A lot this movie.

We are given 27,375 days.

Give or take.

If we are lucky.

I am lucky.

Either way.

🎵 "That first night I was standing at your door." 🎶

And how, in our home at least, and maybe yours too, every story is a love story.

And this is mine.

🎵 "Yeah, I see us in black and white

Crystal clear on a star lit night

In all your gorgeous colors

I promise that I'll love you for the rest of my life." 🎶

Sometimes, you don't want a Hollywood ending.

Because you never want it to end.

To my beautiful, strong, wife.

For giving me everything to ever fight for.

And our story.

I promise.

🍌

entry.title: Gold Magic
entry.summary: If you were a song, what song would you be? I…
entry.content:
If you were a song, what song would you be?

I mean, what's your anthem?

Your theme song?

Your entrance song?

Your exit song?

"When it comes your time to die,

be not like those whose hearts are filled with the fear of death,

so that when their time comes they weep and pray

for a little more time to live their lives

over again in a different way.

Sing your death song

and die like a hero going home."

I mean, we got Sirius, from Alan Parsons.

Something by Tool.

Something by Pac.

Space Lord by Monster Magnet.

The clean version of the song's chorus goes "Space Lord, Mother, Mother."

Which I thought quirky and unique.

Only later did I realize that the original version had a crass word, and they used "Mother" twice instead of "Mother Bleeper."

But you know what?

I like Mother, Mother better.

Space Lord Mother Mother.

So I'd use the clean version.

My wife says the song's dirty.

Even clean.

And I suppose I am, too.

Dirty even clean.

🎵 "Take a sip from my secret potion,

I'll make you fall in love." 🎶

Hey!!!

We should have songs that define us.

Songs that just make us happy.

When we enter a room.

And when we leave it.

And everything in between.

🎵 "I could write it better than you ever felt it." 🎶

Hallelujah.

🎵 "I'm coming out the other side stronger.

One foot in front of the other.

Hands still raising.

Heart still praising." 🎶

Hallelujah, mother mothers.

entry.title: I Was Inverted
entry.summary: Mental models are an interesting thing. Basically, it's like a mini-rulebook…
entry.content:
Mental models are an interesting thing.

Basically, it's like a mini-rulebook on how to think and therefore how to act.

One of note is to invert.

Always invert.

Made famous by Charlie Munger (RIP), you basically start at the end result of what you do not want and work backwards.

Want a good life?

How do you guarantee that you will live a bad one?

Then, do everything you can to avoid the things you listed as living a bad life.

Poor Charlie used to always say, "Where will I pass?"

And then joke that he just would never go to that location, and thus, live forever.

It's an interesting way to think.

Want to be fit?

How do you get fat?

Want to be rich?

How do you stay poor?

Want to be successful?

How do you lose at life?

Sometimes, narrowing it down helps.

What to do based on what not to do.

Next time you're stuck, think about it from a different point of view.

Upside down.

Decide what you do not want.

And give it the finger.

✈️

🛩

entry.title: Late Again
entry.summary: A great actor playing a terrible role once told another actor…
entry.content:
A great actor playing a terrible role once told another actor on screen to "never go full absurd."

He said another word, but that's how I took it.

Never go full absurd.

That's the mistake.

Going maximal absurdity.

Leave a little room for normalcy.

Full absurd makes people squirmy.

Now, I typically enjoy that, but only in degrees.

And thus, believe it or not, I have to dial it down at times.

For the public.

For the column.

In pro wrestling, they say "just be yourself, dialed up to 11."

And in the modern day circus, this advice works.

But I'm just a dude in a spaceship, rocketing around the multiverse.

If I dialed it up to 11, I'd be in on Uranus.

🛸

entry.title: Grand
entry.summary: Workshop with me. Say I get to 60. What will I…
entry.content:
Workshop with me.

Say I get to 60.

What will I be?

Will I be grand?

I hope to be grand.

But not in the way you might think (too soon!).

A mentor.

A founder.

Alive.

The last part is somewhat out of my control.

But I think I can do something about the other two.

I can mentor.

I can found.

I've done both.

I do both.

What does it mean to be a grandmentor?

What does it mean to be a grandfounder?

Mentor of mentors?

Founder of founders?

The cool thing about coining a term is you get to define what it means.

These are the types of things I enjoy doing.

Evoking with words.

Dreaming big.

Playing the long game.

entry.title: Asymmetric Bettor
entry.summary: I don't gamble. At least, not like most. I like little…
entry.content:
I don't gamble.

At least, not like most.

I like little ventures, large outputs.

Now, you're thinking monetarily.

And that's where most people want to take it.

But if you think about it, little ins and big outs are what life is about.

Like a prism.

Light in, rainbow out.

This musings column, I'd like to think, is an asymmetric bet of sorts.

I just sit here and type.

A few minutes a day.

And let me tell you, it has been a huge win.

The trick then is to find your own arenas where a little goes a long way.

Writing.

Coding.

Hustling.

Love.

"You know, sometimes all you need is twenty seconds of insane courage, just literally, twenty seconds of just embarrassing bravery. And I promise you, something great will come of it."

To underdogs.

And underdawgs.

Betting on you.

🐾

entry.title: Vidi, Vici, Veni
entry.summary: You know, I hear they cancelled Latin. I do like a…
entry.content:
You know, I hear they cancelled Latin.

I do like a lot of Latin, though.

It's an interesting language with lots of interesting words and phrases.

But we're not a language class.

We're here to...

[Covers mic.]

What are we here to do, again?

People just come here to read the musings?

No, no, that can't be right.

[Uncovers mic.]

We are here to talk about grace.

About giving it, receiving it, and how it took me ages to understand its power.

For many decades, I never gave it.

I was cold.

Unforgiving.

Ungraceful.

And it worked both ways.

I never gave myself any grace.

Nor did I give it to others.

Unless I didn't care about you. Then you're just invisible.

Ain't that something?

Not giving you grace meant I cared.

Back then.

I'm changed now, right?

Right??

Bah.

Now, respect I did give.

I was raised to respect.

"You're supposed to be my lab partner."

"I am!"

"No, you're not. You're disloyal."

And it's something I did give and did expect.

And if I didn't receive it, well, then there might be a problem.

But I've learned, as I approach these golden years, to extend grace in the face of disrespect.

At least, I know it on paper.

I'm not batting 1.000 here on the extend grace in the face of disrespect stat line.

But we all gotta start somewhere.

Grace falls from the sky like manna from Heaven.

I've lived many ways.

No grace.

Grace.

Everything in between.

Living on grace is a good way to be.

Respectfully,

𖤂

entry.title: Moose Out Front Should Have Told You
entry.summary: Today, I want to talk about love. And how, if you…
entry.content:
Today, I want to talk about love.

And how, if you have it, if you are it, how very little else matters.

Now, I don't want to say that I didn't have it growing up.

I did.

It's just I didn't have it present.

My folks worked so hard.

Every day.

They showed love through their sacrifice.

Toiling so that I might some day reap.

All of that to say, I went searching for love, adventure, and trouble alone.

I suppose we all do.

And I'm blessed to say I found it.

So blessed.

It staggers me.

And while I joke about thinking I'd be a monk alone on some mountain top, a part of me is so shocked it went this way.

A family man?

Beloved?

Daddy?

How about that?

I still worry about a lot of things.

But being loved isn't one of them.

I am loved.

And I know it.

I can see it.

I can feel it.

It's such a unique feeling.

Too powerful to even want to write about.

One I didn't expect I wanted or needed.

But I do.

I need something to fight for.

Someone to fight for.

Always have.

But I never thought I needed love.

It was just something you had.

That is until I met my wife.

Until we made our kids.

Their unconditional love feels to me very conditional.

In that, I have to earn it.

Be worthy of it.

Every single day.

Ahh, probably getting a little sentimental in my old(ish) age.

There's a scene in Pulp Fiction, where Travolta and Jackson are sitting in the diner, discussing what their future holds.

And Sam says he is going to walk the earth.

What you mean walk the earth?

You know, like Caine in Kung Fu.

Travel from town to town.

Meet people.

Get in adventures.

And so that's what I did.

But it was a pretty lonely time.

And now, I suppose I still do it.

But I'm never alone.

I got my pride with me.

🦁🐯😼😻

We roll deep.

All of a sudden I'm Chevy Chase.

On perpetual vacation.

Didn't see that coming.

entry.title: I See, I See
entry.summary: Gather round class. Let's all form a circle. And let's sit…
entry.content:
Gather round class.

Let's all form a circle.

And let's sit down on the floor pillows provided to you.

Today, we discuss a topic near and dear to my heart.

We discuss...

Oh, let me open up some windows, get some fresh air.

He's stalling again. He's out of ideas.

There, let's all take a deep inhale of this wonderful invisible gas.

High quality O2, baby.

Hear those wind chimes? 

Got those last week.

Yep, definitely stalling. He's so weird.

Where was I?

Oh yes, the topic.

Today's topic is on haters.

Eeek. Does he know?

Now, I've tried, relatively speaking, to keep my chin down and keep to myself.

Still got haters.

I was shy.

Still got haters.

I was aloof.

Still got haters.

I tried new armors.

Tried being overly me (cranked up to 11) and just tried to be a shouty salesy guy.

Still got haters.

Tried to do right.

Tried to do good.

Tried to be just.

Still got haters.

Now, I'm just me.

And so class, what I will offer you today, is that likely in this circle right now is a hater.

Take a look around.

Brothers and sisters, and friends, and I see some enemies...

And to them, I say, welcome.

I used to think an enemy to my face is more valuable that a false friend behind me.

I suppose I still believe that.

And I still believe what a good friend told me, decades ago, that if the holes of arse are mad at you, you must be doing something right!

Now I confess some good people have rightfully been upset with me, are upset with me, and I regret those tremendously.

Such as I can.

Such is life.

I could have been kinder.

But to the arses, who I have made upset, well it's time to do a little dance.

In your honor.

...[Five minutes later.]...

Good job, everyone.

Deep breaths.

Hear the chimes.

Now, let's all sit down again.

Take a long drink of water.

Appreciate how good that tastes.

Yum.

Hydration.

Hear your heart thumping.

Like a bass drum.

Now look down.

In front of you all are little hand mirrors.

Pick those up.

And tell me what you see.

Friend?

Or enemy?

👁👁

entry.title: No Problemo
entry.summary: High quality problems are the best problems. These are the type…
entry.content:
High quality problems are the best problems.

These are the type of problems one only has when they have a good life. It presumes a good life.

For example, if you spend time thinking about how human beings could ever become starfaring, it presumes you are likely living a life of abundance. Or at least, you have the ability to live in denial.

Lots of good people are struggling with real problems, I won't call them low-quality, but these stink. How will I pay the bills? How will I get my next meal? Where will I lay my head?

These are heartbreakers.

Soulcrushers.

True problems.

Sometimes, you can catch yourself struggling with a high-quality problem.

It can consume you.

And it's important to realize that these are the problems we strive to have.

Where should we put our money? What shall next week's meals be? Should we move?

How do we interstellar travel?

All high-quality problems.

Ones to seek.

May all your problems be high af.
